purpose: Make RLM classification robust to weak individual subagent judgment by requiring evidence packets, prompt-shape scoping, probes, adversarial review, and deterministic gate adjudication.
core_principle: Subagents are evidence collectors and adversarial analysts, not authorities. The final verdict should come from reproducible evidence plus a deterministic gate adjudication.
evidence_hierarchy: Observed black-box traces from real runs.
Generated scripts, runtime records, subagent transcripts, and tool logs.
Source code paths that implement runtime, prompt assembly, context handling, or subcall primitives.
Maintainer claims, names, tweets, README slogans, and product positioning.

prompt_shape_matrix.symbolic_context: User text or conversation text, if bound into runtime state by handle.
File path or file object referenced from the prompt, such as @file.
Repo path, directory path, file list, search index, or code database handle.
Named context fields, object fields, database rows, IDs, handles, or blobs.
Transcript path, message object, conversation handle, or event log.
BM25/vector index handle, corpus IDs, document IDs, or queryable store.

rules: Use unknown when a gate was not tested.
Use conditional when a product passes only for some configurations or prompt shapes.
Every pass must cite observed evidence or source paths.
Every fail must include the strongest plausible contrary interpretation.
A product-level verdict must summarize all tested run shapes, not collapse them into one label.
anti_skill_issue_design: Calibration fixtures before judging real targets.
Evidence packet first, verdict prose second.
Two-pass adversarial review from the same evidence.
Provenance ledger for commands, run IDs, versions, costs, and artifact paths.
Escalation for disagreement, missing probes, or uncited gate passes.
